target.name: Liquid AI
target.founder: Ramin Hasani, Mathias Lechner, Alexander Amini, Daniela Rus

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $2.4B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $400.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Palantir
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $400B+

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Twelve Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $250M

data.narrative:
Liquid AI is a Series A-stage AI & software company founded by Ramin Hasani, Mathias Lechner, Alexander Amini, and Daniela Rus, currently valued at $2.35B post-money on $287M total raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables (Field AI at $2.0B, Twelve Labs at $250M, and Palantir at $400.0B), supplemented by a broader private frontier-tech universe including OpenAI and xAI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, anchoring to the $2.4B last post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage progression, implies $4.7B. The peer-median method across the three same-stage comparables yields $2.0B, though the peer set exhibits extreme dispersion ($250M to $400.0B), reflecting heterogeneity in AI & software outcomes. The wide range reflects the early-stage nature of this sector and absence of revenue or profitability benchmarks.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.7B (low) to $5.4B (high), with a median of $3.4B. The current $2.35B valuation sits slightly below the median implied value. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set—particularly the outsized influence of Palantir—and the lack of product-market fit or revenue metrics to anchor relative value. Stage-progression methodology carries material risk given AI sector volatility and founder-dependent returns.
